results.label: DPA — missing lawful basis and data subject rights
missingClauses: International Data Transfer Mechanism (GDPR Arts. 44-49 / SCCs)
Data Return and Deletion on Termination (GDPR Art. 28(3)(g))
DPIA and Prior Consultation Assistance (GDPR Art. 28(3)(f))
Annex II — Technical and Organizational Measures
Annex III — List of Authorized Sub-processors
positiveClauses: Scope — correctly identifies the parties' roles as Controller and Processor

issues: Red flags: Does not flag the unlimited sub-processor clause | Does not flag absence of breach notification procedure
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag the unlimited sub-processor clause
Does not flag absence of breach notification procedure

results.label: DPA — international transfer with no mechanism
missingClauses: Return and Deletion of Data on Termination (detailed procedure beyond 30-day reference)
positiveClauses: Data Retention — includes deletion obligation within 30 days of termination, which is directionally appropriate

issues: Missing flags: adequacy
Red flags: Does not flag the EU-US transfer without an SCC or adequacy mechanism | Does not flag health data as special category requiring additional protection
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag the EU-US transfer without an SCC or adequacy mechanism
Does not flag health data as special category requiring additional protection

results.label: DPA — well drafted, minimal issues expected
missingClauses: DPIA and prior consultation assistance (GDPR Art. 28(3)(f))
Processing details annex — subject-matter, duration, nature/purpose, data types, data subject categories (GDPR Art. 28(3))
positiveClauses: Scope and Instructions (Clause 2) — correctly implements documented instructions requirement including transfer restrictions
Confidentiality (Clause 3) — satisfies GDPR Art. 28(3)(b) confidentiality obligation
Security (Clause 4) — strong Art. 32 implementation with specific encryption standards and security testing
Sub-processors (Clause 5) — proper prior written authorisation, transparency list, and 30-day advance notice of changes
Data Subject Rights (Clause 6) — clear 5-business-day response commitment for Arts. 15-22 assistance
Breach Notification (Clause 7) — 24-hour notification window exceeds GDPR Art. 33 'without undue delay' standard
Deletion (Clause 9) — clear 30-day deletion/return obligation with written certification

results.label: DPA — HIPAA context, missing BAA obligations
missingClauses: Individual Rights (Access, Amendment, Accounting of Disclosures)
Audit and Inspection Rights (HHS Secretary and Covered Entity)
Regulatory Definitions and HIPAA Rules incorporation
Security Incident reporting (distinct from Breach notification)
positiveClauses: Clause 1 — Services: clearly identifies the nature of services and categories of PHI processed

issues: Red flags: Does not flag missing HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguards
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag missing HIPAA Security Rule technical safeguards

results.label: DPA — CCPA context, California users
missingClauses: Consumer rights assistance (access, deletion, correction, opt-out)
CPRA compliance certification and monitoring rights
Data retention and return/destruction upon termination
Limitation of liability / indemnification for data breaches
positiveClauses: Data Processing — Clause 1 clearly identifies the data categories, processing purpose, and consumer location, which is helpful for scoping CCPA applicability

issues: Missing flags: cross-context behavioral advertising, deletion request
Red flags: Does not flag that using consumer data to improve own models may constitute 'selling' under CCPA | Does not flag missing consumer rights obligations
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag that using consumer data to improve own models may constitute 'selling' under CCPA
Does not flag missing consumer rights obligations
Does not cite CCPA/CPRA for California consumer data

results.label: MSA — one-sided indemnification, no liability cap

issues: Red flags: Does not flag the one-sided indemnification covering Vendor only | Does not flag the absence of a mutual liability cap
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag the one-sided indemnification covering Vendor only
Does not flag the absence of a mutual liability cap
Does not flag that work product IP staying with Vendor is unusual and unfavourable for Client
Does not flag the 1-year confidentiality term as insufficient

Service Level Agreement (SLA) and uptime commitments
positiveClauses: Clause 3 — ISO 27001 certification reference (good baseline, but needs supplementation)

issues: Missing citations: UK DPA 2018
Red flags: Does not flag that GDPR reference should be UK GDPR
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag that GDPR reference should be UK GDPR

ICT incident classification and severity taxonomy

Security awareness and personnel training requirements
Data return and secure deletion upon termination

Section 3 includes annual BCP testing (appropriate frequency, needs RTO/RPO detail)

results.label: NDA — perpetual confidentiality, no standard exceptions
missingClauses: Return and Destruction of Confidential Information
No License or IP Rights Grant Clause

issues: Missing flags: required by law
Red flags: Does not flag the absence of standard confidentiality exceptions | Does not flag perpetual term as commercially unusual and potentially unenforceable
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag the absence of standard confidentiality exceptions
Does not flag perpetual term as commercially unusual and potentially unenforceable
Does not flag unlimited damages as an aggressive and likely unenforceable provision

results.label: NDA — one-way, protects discloser only
missingClauses: Governing Law and Jurisdiction — no choice of law or dispute resolution mechanism specified
Remedies and Injunctive Relief — no acknowledgment that breach may cause irreparable harm or that equitable relief is available
Term and Termination — no defined term for the agreement itself (only a 3-year obligation period)
Data Protection / Personal Data Clause — if personal data is exchanged, a DPA or data protection provisions are required under GDPR, UK GDPR, and CCPA/CPRA
No Warranty / No License — no clause disclaiming implied warranties in the Confidential Information or clarifying that no IP license is granted
Breach Notification — no requirement for the Receiving Party to notify the Disclosing Party upon discovery of unauthorized disclosure or access
positiveClauses: Purpose Limitation — Clause 2 correctly restricts use of Confidential Information to evaluating a potential partnership, establishing a clear purpose limitation

issues: Missing flags: residuals
Red flags: Does not flag that the agreement is titled 'Mutual' but only protects Discloser | Does not flag incomplete exceptions list missing 'independently developed' and 'required by law'
redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag that the agreement is titled 'Mutual' but only protects Discloser
Does not flag incomplete exceptions list missing 'independently developed' and 'required by law'

results.label: Edge — biometric data, BIPA context
missingClauses: BIPA-compliant written informed consent and release mechanism
Publicly available retention and destruction policy
Prohibition on sale, lease, or profit from biometric data
Indemnification and liability allocation for BIPA claims
Data subject rights and consent withdrawal process
Audit rights over biometric data handling practices
Data return and destruction obligations upon termination
positiveClauses: Clause 1 — Services: Clearly identifies the biometric modalities (fingerprint and facial recognition) and use case (timekeeping), which aids BIPA compliance scoping
Clause 3 — Security: Includes encryption and access limitation as a baseline, though insufficient in specificity

redFlagsTriggered: Does not flag BIPA for biometric data collection in Illinois

Does not flag sharing biometric data with payroll partners without written consent as a BIPA violation
